name: OpenSSF Scorecard
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); REST API via api.securityscorecards.dev (free, no auth for reads)

auth.notes: Public API requires no auth for reads; GitHub token required for private repo scanning; OpenSSF API rate limited

performance.notes: Cached results via api.securityscorecards.dev ~300ms; live analysis can take minutes

agent_readiness.notes: Google/OSSF's automated security assessment for open source projects. Scores 20+ security practices: branch protection, code review, signed releases, vulnerability reporting, dependency updates, token permissions, etc. Free public REST API for querying cached scores. CLI for running locally. GitHub Actions integration. Used by thousands of OSS projects.

gotchas: Public API only has data for repos already scanned — may miss newer/smaller projects
Score interpretation requires understanding each check's meaning
Some checks require GitHub token with elevated permissions
Live scanning is slow (minutes per repo); cached API is fast but potentially stale
best_when: Agents evaluating open source dependencies for supply chain risk
CI/CD pipelines auditing third-party dependencies before adoption
Security teams wanting automated OSS security posture scoring
Developer portals showing security health of dependencies
avoid_when: Real-time vulnerability detection (Grype/Trivy better for CVE scanning)
summary: OpenSSF Scorecard automatically assesses security practices of open source GitHub repositories across 20+ checks (branch protection, code review, signed releases, etc.). Free public REST API for querying cached scores. CLI for local analysis. Used for supply chain risk assessment of OSS dependencies. Best for agents evaluating dependency security before adoption. GitHub-centric; limited for non-GitHub projects.
